title: Design for low-power operation
description: Drive the CPU into sleep/stop modes between events and wake on interrupt, instead of busy-waiting, to meet battery and thermal budgets.
body: Use when the device is battery-powered or thermally constrained. Do not busy-wait or poll in a loop when you could sleep, and do not leave peripherals/clocks enabled when idle. Actions: use an event-driven design that enters the deepest safe sleep mode and wakes on interrupt, gate unused peripheral clocks, and use a tickless RTOS idle if available; measure current in each mode. Validation signals include a measured average current that meets the battery-life budget and correct wake latency from each sleep mode. Risk boundary: deep sleep can disable peripherals or memory retention and break wake timing; verify wake sources and state retention on hardware before relying on a sleep mode in the field.
